title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
At the Chimney House, a young man named Jimmy Sycamore runs down the stairs and collides with the butler, Mr. Truddwell. Jimmy arrives late for breakfast at 11:15 AM, drawing a reproachful look from the landlady, Mrs. Cutt. Mrs. Cutt, a tall and beautiful woman with a sorrowful demeanor, views tardiness as a significant sin due to her past marriage to Sir Osbert Cutt, who demanded strict regular habits. Although she has achieved high social status through Sir Osbert's success, she feels isolated in the large, rented mansion known as Smokestack Villa.

Seeking interaction, Mrs. Cutt approaches the head gardener, McDonald, on the terrace. She first asks to pick ripe grapes for dessert, but McDonald firmly informs her they are not ready. She then proposes converting the lawn behind the rose garden into a croquet lawn for Sir Osbert's enjoyment. McDonald refuses, using the prospect of involving "William" as a deterrent to coerce her into dropping the suggestion. Mrs. Cutt quickly surrenders to his authority.

After finishing a meal of kidney and bacon, Jimmy joins Mrs. Cutt on the terrace. They discuss the whereabouts of the other residents, specifically noting that Mr. Wade has not yet come down for breakfast. Mr. Truddwell notes that Mr. Wade did not arrive until 11:30 PM the previous day. As Mrs. Cutt expresses concern over the lateness, a serious young man wearing glasses enters the room.
